rulings.Case Title: PRECILA BALABBO VS. CRICUT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Bifurcation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2024, Line 1, DEFENDANT CRICUT, INC.'s Amended Motion For Bifurcation. The motion to bifurcate is TRANSFERRED to Department 206 on the day of trial, January 27, 2025, to be heard by the assigned trial judge. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/CK).

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDRA ROGERS VS. ESHARES INC. D/B/A CARTA, INC., A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2024, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ALEXANDRA ROGERS' Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint. (The full tentative ruling emailed to the parties). Plaintiff Alexandra Rogers's motion for leave to amend her complaint is denied. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/CK).
